content:
# elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram Daily Update (Apr 25, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on April 25, 2026, focused on improving the stability of the Telegram plugin. The primary achievement was the implementation of a mechanism to prevent duplicate pollers per bot token, ensuring more reliable runtime performance and avoiding conflicts during service restarts.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**Poller Management and Stability**
* Implemented tracking for active pollers per bot token to prevent duplicate instances ([#27](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram/pull/27)).
* Added logic to stop or replace existing pollers before launching new runtime instances ([#27](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram/pull/27)).
* Enabled clean deregistration of pollers upon service stop to prevent resource contention ([#27](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram/pull/27)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
* None

## CLOSED ISSUES
* None

## NEW ISSUES
* None
